description.undefined: A formal OWL 2 DL ontology for representing academic research publications, authors, venues, institutions, citation relationships, funding, and open access status.

changeNote.value: v2.0: Added ResearchGroup, Grant, FundingBody, AffiliationRecord, OpenAccessStatus, PeerReviewRecord, Preprint classes. Added 6 new object properties and 4 new data properties. Modelling follows MOMo methodology with record and controlled-vocabulary patterns.

comment.undefined: A subject area or thematic domain of academic research.
A dedicated research organisation, not necessarily degree-granting.
The top-level class for all types of academic research outputs.

labels.undefined: Knowledge Engineering Lab, Example University A

comment.undefined: A named research group or laboratory operating within a university or research institute.
A peer-reviewed article published in a scholarly journal.
A funded research grant associated with one or more publications.
A record capturing the peer-review status of a publication, including whether it was reviewed and at which venue. Follows the MOMo provenance pattern.
A channel or forum where academic research is formally published or presented.
A doctoral dissertation or master's thesis submitted to an academic institution.
An organisation with which researchers may be affiliated.
A chapter or section published within an edited academic book.
A formal reference from one publication to another.
A person who conducts academic research.
A human being involved in academic research activities.
A higher-education institution that conducts research.
A version of a research paper made publicly available before formal peer review, typically deposited on a preprint server such as arXiv or bioRxiv.
Free to read on the publisher site but without an open licence.
Immediately freely available via the publisher under an open licence.
Self-archived in a repository (preprint or accepted manuscript).
Access is restricted; requires subscription or payment.
The open access category of a publication. Controlled vocabulary: gold, green, bronze, closed. Follows the MOMo controlled-vocabulary (purple-box) pattern.
A paper accepted and presented at an academic conference or workshop.
A time-bounded record linking an Author to an Institution for a given period. Follows the MOMo record pattern, which reifies relationships that change over time.
An industrial or commercial organisation engaged in research.
A formal report describing the results or progress of technical research.
A periodical publication containing peer-reviewed academic articles.
A smaller, more focused academic event often co-located with a major conference.
An organisation responsible for producing and distributing publications.
A researcher who has authored one or more publications.
An organisation that provides research funding, such as a national science foundation, charity, or government agency.
A term or phrase assigned to a publication to describe its content.
An academic event at which research papers are presented and published in proceedings.
A researcher who peer-reviews academic manuscripts.
The year in which the affiliation described by this record began.
The open access type label. Expected values: gold, green, bronze, closed.
Relates an author to a time-bounded affiliation record. Implements the MOMo record pattern to allow tracking of affiliation changes over time.
Relates two authors who have co-authored at least one publication together.
The full title of the publication.
The International Standard Serial Number of the journal.
Relates an author to a publication they have authored.
Relates a publication to one of its authors.
The textual label of the keyword.
The official award or grant number assigned by the funding body.
Direct affiliation link between a researcher and an institution (simplified role-chain shortcut, following Norouzi et al. 2025).
Relates a publication to a grant that funded the research.
The last page number of the publication.
The full name of the person.
Relates a grant to the funding body that issued it.
The Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCID) of the researcher.
The first page number of the publication.
The journal volume in which the article appears.
Relates a publication to its open access category (gold, green, bronze, or closed).
The year in which the affiliation described by this record ended. Omitted if the affiliation is current.
The year in which the publication was published.
Relates a publication to one of its descriptor keywords.
The name of the research topic.
Relates a publication to publications that cite it.
Relates a publication to the publisher responsible for it.
Relates a publication to the venue in which it was published.
Relates a publication to another publication it cites.
Relates a publication to a research topic it covers.
Relates a publication to a record of its peer-review status.
Relates an institution to a researcher affiliated with it.
The Digital Object Identifier (DOI) of the publication.
The journal issue in which the article appears.
Relates an author to a research group they belong to.
The total number of times the publication has been cited.
The abstract or summary text of the publication.
Relates a keyword to the broader research topic it represents.
The institution named in an affiliation record.
The current Journal Impact Factor (JIF) of the journal.
